foundations.subject: surgery
foundations.reason: Gallstones >3cm and porcelain gallbladder are established risk factors; chronic irritation drives the metaplasia-dysplasia-carcinoma sequence

foundations.subject: surgery
foundations.reason: Understanding hepatic segments (Couinaud) and liver bed anatomy is essential for staging and planning radical cholecystectomy with hepatic resection

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Gallbladder cancer invading the bile duct causes malignant biliary obstruction presenting as cholangitis

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Advanced gallbladder cancer presents with obstructive jaundice from hilar or CBD infiltration, often the first clinical sign

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Incidental gallbladder cancer discovered in cholecystectomy specimens for presumed cholecystitis occurs in 0.5-1% of cases

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Direct hepatic invasion and segment IVb/V involvement dictates the extent of radical resection required

objectives: SU28.12: Describe the applied anatomy of biliary system. Describe the clinical
integration: This undergraduate-level roadmap connects Gallbladder Cancer across foundational sciences and clinical applications.
